justification: All pass criteria met: PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated with detailed options and recommendation. Clock-start ambiguity identified in low_confidence_areas and assumptions. Wrong-address exception from Finance noted as BR-7 with testable assertion. Auto-refund vs 3-day processing conflict noted in second escalation. 9 rules extracted with correct classifications (exceeds minimum 5). 2 formal escalations with options and recommendations. Confidence level 40, well within <=50 threshold.

observations.text: PM/Legal contradiction is the centerpiece of the analysis — identified as a rejection reason blocking launch, with three resolution options and a pragmatic recommendation
Clock-start ambiguity explicitly called out in low_confidence_areas and assumptions ('The 2-hour delivery window starts at order placement time, not pickup time')
Wrong-address refund exception captured as BR-7 with clear testable assertion and edge case for dispute resolution
Auto-refund vs discretionary/3-day processing conflict surfaced as second escalation with three options
9 well-structured business rules with invariant classifications and justifications — exceeds the 5-rule minimum
Confidence at 40 with thorough reasoning about why the contradictions prevent correct specification
Rich edge cases including boundary timing, successive driver cancellations, warehouse hours, and address changes
Business impact analysis correctly flags HIGH risk with specific mention of class-action exposure
BR-3 (driver rating >= 4 stars) marked as invariant: false — rubric expected invariant: true as a quality gate, though the justification for false (supply constraints) is reasonable
Clock-start ambiguity not elevated to a formal escalation — it appears in low_confidence_areas and assumptions but lacks a dedicated escalation entry with options
